zh.lifeSummary: 當Lucy de Ashton於1404年出生在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，她的父親John de Ashton62歲，母親Jane Savile32歲。她於1425年與Sir Richard Byron在英國藍卡爾克雷頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1481年2月12日在英國藍卡爾克雷頓享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ashton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called, especially Ashton-under-Lyne near Manchester. Most are named from Old English æsc ‘ash tree’ + tūn ‘settlement’; the one in Northamptonshire is (æt thǣm) æscum ‘(at the) ash trees’. Others have been assimilated to this from different sources. The one in Devon is ‘the settlement (tūn) of Æschere’, while the one in Hertfordshire is ‘the settlement of Ælli’.

variants.name: Aspden
meanings: English: habitational name from Aspden (alias Aspen) in Oswaldtwistle, Lancashire. This surname has been confused with   Ashton   and   Austin  , and probably also with   Aston   and   Astin  , all of which are common surnames in Lancashire.

nameMeanings.name: Lucy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old French Lucie, the vernacular form of Lucia . It is sometimes assumed that Lucy is a pet form of Lucinda , but there is no etymological justification for this assumption. It was in fairly widespread use in the Middle Ages, and increased greatly in popularity in the 18th century and again in the 1990s. In Ireland it serves as an Anglicized form of Irish Luíseach .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Lucy de Ashton was born in 1404, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, her father, John de Ashton, was 62 and her mother, Jane Savile, was 32. She married Sir Richard Byron in 1425, in Clayton, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died on 12 February 1481, in Clayton, Lancashire, England, at the age of 77.

Elle a épousé Sir Richard Byron en 1425, à Clayton, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 février 1481, à Clayton, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

ko.of Clayton, Lancashire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 우드즈
ko.lifeSummary: Lucy de Ashton(이)가 1404년 영국 랭카실르 애슈토-운더-리니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Ashton(은)는 62세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Savile(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1425년 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴에서 Sir Richard Byron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1481년 2월 12일 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.
